visible_observations: A tall oval pod-like body is wrapped in overlapping pale cracked plates separated by dark braided or fleshy channels and supported by two short squared legs.
The central opening contains a bound cocoon form with a concentric toothed mouth, while a tapered upper structure carries a small vertical purple slit.
The layered shell, enclosed mouth, and compact leg arrangement create a specific nonhuman construction.
defects: The nearly perfect bilateral symmetry and upright frontal stance make the figure appear static and sealed rather than actively predatory.
No visible arms, weapon, loaded limb, or directional action explains how the body attacks or interacts in combat.
The bound central form can read as a captive or helpless contained creature, competing with the threat of the enclosing body.
Repeated pale plate rows dominate the design while the dark interior anatomy is compressed into narrow gaps.
uncertainties: The image does not make clear whether the purple slit is an eye, whether the toothed cocoon is the primary face, or whether two beings are present.
The cords around the central form could indicate restraint, protection, incubation, or anatomy; their visible purpose is unresolved.
Scale and locomotion cannot be inferred from the isolated frontal presentation.

visible_observations: Three black candidates are shown on parchment: a very tall narrow leaf or spear pod on two legs, a low wide radial shell on multiple articulated legs, and an oval split pod on two legs with an exposed central form.
Height, width, locomotion, negative space, and degree of enclosure differ substantially among the three candidates.
defects: The tall and oval candidates share the same layered leaf-shell language, so their outer contours differ more in proportion than in underlying construction.
The low candidate's large black central cavity and dense leg cluster obscure a clear focal anatomy.
The two-legged candidates read first as seed pods or architectural shells; no attack limb or directional weapon shape breaks that passive outline.
uncertainties: The A, B, and C labels do not explicitly state which candidate was selected.
Solid black treatment prevents evaluation of whether the internal mouth, eye, and binding details clarify the locomotion and threat ambiguity.

visible_observations: The enlargement shows a lower cocoon-like form tightly crossed by cords, with a vertical mouth composed of nested teeth and dark inner folds.
Above it, a separate tapered armored structure contains a narrow purple slit, while pale shell plates close around both forms.
defects: The purple slit and toothed opening compete as separate facial focal points, leaving the viewer uncertain where the unit's attention originates.
The mouth communicates hunger, pain, or screaming through teeth alone but shows no directional gaze, asymmetry, or changing expression.
The centered symmetry and enclosure provide little visible evidence of a specific current intent beyond containment.
uncertainties: Unaided viewing cannot determine whether the lower form is the unit, an offspring, a prisoner, or an organ.
It is unclear whether the purple slit is sensory anatomy or ornament, so the active face mechanism remains unresolved.

visible_observations: At native size, the light oval shell, dark diagonal plate gaps, central dark opening, and two short legs remain visible against the dark square.
The image reads primarily as a compact upright pod or layered seed shape.
defects: The toothed mouth, binding cords, purple slit, blood marks, and cracked material detail collapse at this size.
The small dark interior does not resolve as a face, captive form, or attack mechanism.
The surviving pod silhouette communicates enclosure more strongly than motion, hostility, or role.
uncertainties: No neighboring unit icons, gameplay background, selection frame, or motion state are present, so in-context recognition and threat readability cannot be determined.
